privacy.article1Content:
Coffee Ledger (hereinafter "Service") processes personal information for the following purposes. Personal information collected will not be used for purposes other than those stated below. If the purpose of use changes, necessary measures such as obtaining separate consent will be taken in accordance with Article 18 of the Personal Information Protection Act (PIPA).

a. Member registration and management: Verification of intent to register, identification and authentication for membership services, maintenance and management of member status, prevention of unauthorized use, delivery of notices and notifications
b. Service provision: Management of in-house coffee expenses, recording of orders, balance management, deposit management
c. Notification service: Sending push notifications (FCM) with member consent
privacy.article2Title: Article 2 (Personal Information Collected and Collection Methods)
privacy.article2Content:
The Service collects the following personal information for registration and service use.

a. Required items: Email address, name (nickname), password (stored encrypted)
b. Automatically collected items: Service usage records, access logs, cookies, IP address, device information
c. Optional items: FCM token (when push notification consent is given)

Collection methods: Direct input by users during registration, automatic generation and collection during service use
privacy.article3Content:
The Service processes and retains personal information within the retention and usage period prescribed by law or agreed upon at the time of collection.

a. Member information: Until membership withdrawal. However, information will be retained until the end of the applicable period in the following cases:
  - Under the Act on Consumer Protection in Electronic Commerce: Records of contracts or withdrawal of offers for 5 years, records of payment and supply of goods for 5 years, records of consumer complaints or dispute resolution for 3 years
  - Under the Protection of Communications Secrets Act: Website visit records for 3 months
b. Service usage records (orders, deposit history, etc.): Until membership withdrawal or until the purpose of retention is achieved
privacy.article4Content:
The Service processes personal information only within the scope of purposes specified in Article 1, and provides personal information to third parties only in cases falling under Articles 17 and 18 of the Personal Information Protection Act, such as with the consent of the data subject or under special provisions of law.

Currently, the Service does not provide users' personal information to any third parties.
privacy.article5Content:
The Service outsources the processing of personal information as follows for smooth operations.

a. Data storage and management: Supabase Inc. (cloud database and authentication services)
b. Push notification delivery: Google LLC (Firebase Cloud Messaging)
c. Web hosting: Vercel Inc. (web application hosting)

If the content of outsourced work or the outsourced party changes, it will be disclosed through this Privacy Policy without delay.
privacy.article6Title: Article 6 (Rights and Obligations of Data Subjects)
privacy.article6Content:
Data subjects may exercise the following rights regarding their personal information at any time.

a. Request to access personal information
b. Request for correction of errors
c. Request for deletion
d. Request to suspend processing

These rights may be exercised through the settings menu within the Service, or by contacting the Service operator via email. The Service will take action without delay in response to such requests.

If a data subject requests correction or deletion of personal information due to errors, the Service will not use or provide the relevant personal information until the correction or deletion is completed.
privacy.article7Content:
The Service destroys personal information without delay when it is no longer needed, such as when the retention period has expired or the purpose of processing has been achieved.

a. Destruction procedure: Unnecessary personal information is destroyed with approval from the privacy officer.
b. Destruction method: Information in electronic file format is deleted using technical methods that prevent the records from being reproduced.
privacy.article8Content:
The Service takes the following measures to ensure the security of personal information.

a. Password encryption: Member passwords are encrypted for storage and management, so only the member can know their password.
b. Technical measures against hacking: Security programs are installed with regular updates and inspections, and systems are installed in access-controlled areas with technical and physical monitoring and blocking.
c. Access restriction to personal information: Access rights to the database systems processing personal information are granted, modified, and revoked to implement necessary access control measures.
d. Data transmission encryption: All data is encrypted and transmitted through SSL/TLS protocols.
e. Row Level Security (RLS): Row-level security policies are applied at the database level to ensure access only to authorized data.
privacy.article9Content:
The Service uses cookies to store and retrieve usage information for providing personalized services.

a. Purpose of cookies: Maintaining login sessions, managing user authentication status
b. Installation and rejection of cookies: You may refuse cookie storage through web browser settings. However, refusing cookies may cause difficulties in using services that require login.
c. Push notifications: The Service provides push notification functionality through FCM (Firebase Cloud Messaging), which is sent only with separate member consent. Members may refuse to receive notifications at any time through browser settings or Service settings.
privacy.article10Content:
The Service has designated a privacy officer as follows to oversee personal information processing and to handle complaints and damage relief related to personal information processing.

Privacy Officer
- Contact: Service operator
- Email: dww7541@gmail.com

Data subjects may direct all inquiries, complaints, and damage relief matters related to personal information protection arising from the use of the Service to the privacy officer. You may also reach us through Settings > Contact within the Service.
privacy.article11Content:
The Service stores personal information on servers outside Korea or outsources processing to overseas entities as follows.

a. Supabase Inc. (United States): Database storage and authentication services
b. Google LLC (United States): Push notification delivery via Firebase Cloud Messaging
c. Vercel Inc. (United States): Web application hosting

The items of personal information transferred, purpose of transfer, and retention period are as described in the relevant provisions of this Policy. These companies manage information securely in accordance with their respective privacy policies.
privacy.article12Content:
This Privacy Policy takes effect from the effective date. When additions, deletions, or corrections are made due to changes in laws or policies, the changes will be announced through in-service notices at least 7 days before the effective date of the changes.

This Policy is effective as of March 17, 2026.
privacy.article13Content:
Data subjects may apply for dispute resolution or consultation with the following organizations for relief from personal information infringement.

a. Personal Information Dispute Mediation Committee: 1833-6972 (www.kopico.go.kr)
b. Personal Information Infringement Report Center (KISA): 118 (privacy.kisa.or.kr)
c. Supreme Prosecutors' Office: 1301 (www.spo.go.kr)
d. National Police Agency: 182 (ecrm.cyber.go.kr)

terms.article1Content: These Terms govern the conditions, procedures, rights, obligations, and responsibilities between the Service provider and members, as well as other necessary matters regarding the use of the in-house coffee expense management service provided by Coffee Ledger (hereinafter "Service").
terms.article2Content:
The definitions of terms used in these Terms are as follows.

a. "Service" refers to the web application for managing in-house coffee expenses, providing functions such as deposit management per member, balance management, order recording, and history inquiry.
b. "Member" refers to a person who has agreed to these Terms and registered for the Service.
c. "Operator" refers to the person who operates and manages the Service.
d. "Company" refers to the organizational unit that jointly manages coffee expenses within the Service.
terms.article3Content:
a. These Terms apply to all members who wish to use the Service.
b. These Terms take effect when posted on the Service screen or otherwise notified to members, and when members agree and register for the Service.
c. The Service may modify these Terms within the scope that does not violate applicable laws when reasonable grounds arise. When Terms are modified, the changes will be announced through in-service notices at least 7 days before the effective date.
d. If a member does not agree to the modified Terms, they may discontinue use and withdraw. Continued use of the Service after the effective date of the modified Terms shall be deemed consent to the modified Terms.
terms.article4Content:
a. The use agreement is formed when a person wishing to become a member agrees to the Terms, applies for registration, and the Service approves it.
b. Registration is completed through email verification. Members must provide accurate information at registration, and providing false information may result in restricted use.
c. The Service may refuse or subsequently terminate the use agreement in the following cases:
  1) Using another person's identity
  2) Providing false information or failing to provide required information
  3) Other cases where the Service's registration requirements are not met
terms.article5Content:
a. Members may request withdrawal from the Service at any time, and the Service will process the withdrawal immediately.
b. Upon withdrawal, related data will be processed in accordance with the Privacy Policy. However, information may be retained for the period required by applicable laws.
c. If a member engages in any of the following, the Service may restrict or suspend membership:
  1) Registering false information during registration
  2) Using the Service to engage in acts prohibited by law or these Terms
  3) Interfering with other members' use of the Service or stealing their information
  4) Using the Service to engage in acts contrary to public order and morals
terms.article6Content:
a. Members must comply with applicable laws, these Terms, usage guidelines, and notices related to the Service.
b. Members must use the Service only for the purpose of managing in-house coffee expenses.
c. Members must not engage in the following:
  1) Registering false information during registration or modification
  2) Stealing another person's information
  3) Altering information posted on the Service
  4) Transmitting or posting information (computer programs, etc.) other than information designated by the Service
  5) Intentionally or negligently interfering with the operation of the Service
  6) Other acts in violation of applicable laws
d. Members are responsible for securely managing their account credentials (email, password) and must not allow third parties to use them.
terms.article7Content:
a. The Service performs the following functions:
  1) In-house coffee expense management (deposit and balance management per member)
  2) Order recording and history inquiry
  3) Calendar-based order status viewing
  4) Excel export
  5) Push notification service (for consenting members only)
b. The Service is provided free of charge.
c. The Service will announce any changes to the service content.
terms.article8Content:
a. The Service may temporarily suspend service provision due to maintenance, replacement, or malfunction of information and communication equipment, or interruption of communications.
b. The Service may restrict or temporarily suspend service provision due to force majeure such as natural disasters.
c. When service provision is temporarily suspended, prior notice will be given. However, post-notification may be made when prior notice is not possible due to unavoidable circumstances.
d. If the Service intends to terminate, it will notify members at least 30 days before the termination date.
terms.article9Content:
a. The Service will not engage in acts prohibited by applicable laws and these Terms or contrary to public morals, and will do its best to provide continuous and stable service.
b. The Service will publish and comply with a Privacy Policy to protect members' personal information.
c. The Service will make efforts to address opinions or complaints raised by members regarding service use when they are deemed legitimate.
terms.article10Content:
a. The Service is exempt from responsibility for service provision when unable to provide service due to force majeure such as natural disasters.
b. The Service is not responsible for service disruptions caused by the member's own fault.
c. The Service is not responsible for any expected profits that members fail to obtain or lose through using the Service.
d. Amounts managed within the Service (deposits, balances, etc.) are recording tools representing actual monetary values, and the Service bears no obligation for monetary guarantees or refunds.
e. As the Service is provided free of charge, the Service does not bear additional liability for damages related to service use beyond the responsibilities prescribed by applicable laws such as the Personal Information Protection Act.
terms.article11Content:
a. Disputes between the Service and members regarding service use shall be resolved by mutual agreement.
b. If a lawsuit is filed due to a dispute between the Service and a member regarding service use, Korean law shall be the governing law, and the case shall be filed with the competent court under the Civil Procedure Act.
terms.article12Content: These Terms are effective as of March 17, 2026.
